> Soil comes first—  
> not because it’s pretty,  
> but because it can carry the seed without lying.  
> You don’t plant in air.  
> You plant in what’s been turned, churned,  
> where rot had its time and dark had a say.  
>

> Not all of them serve.

> And you’ll feel it,  
> even if you can’t explain why it moves you.  
>  
> Prioritizing?  
> Start with the ground that says yes.  
> That says *feed me*,  
> not the ground that just looks fertile from far off.  
>  
> What eludes you might not be yours to chase.  
> What stays…  
> what stares back through the stalks  
> might be what you planted before you were born.”
